title: InstalledPlugins::load silently resets on parse failure — data-loss-on-corruption
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/plugins/install.rs:110-135
## Violation
A corrupt or unparseable installed_plugins.json is treated identically to "file missing": both fall back to Default. Quoted lines:

    Err(e) => {
        warn!(error = %e, "Failed to parse installed_plugins.json, starting fresh");
        Self::default()
    }
    ...
    Err(e) => {
        warn!(error = %e, "Failed to read installed_plugins.json");
        Self::default()
    }

The next save() then overwrites the corrupt file with an empty registry — every prior install record is gone. The user gets a single `warn!` line and loses their plugin inventory.
## Mandated refactor
On parse failure, rename the bad file to installed_plugins.json.broken-<timestamp> (or .bak) BEFORE returning Default. Surface a top-level error (or `Result<Self, PluginError>`) so the CLI can prompt the user instead of silently losing data. Add a schema version check that refuses to overwrite a file whose `version` field is newer than the deserialiser supports.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
